methods.method_name: Sediment Core Retrieval and Initial Processing
methods.description: Retrieval of sediment cores using a Rumohr corer, followed by initial X-ray photography and image merging for documentation.

steps.description: Two sediment cores, ACDC2014-009 (110 cm long) and ACDC2014-010 (89 cm long), were retrieved from a water depth of 280 m (69N 45.513/50W 44.996) from R/V Porsild, on 31 August 2014, using a Rumohr corer with 2-m core liners.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve sediment cores from specified location and depth using a Rumohr corer

steps.description: The cores were photographed using a YXLON smart 160 E/0.4 system (120 s, 3 mA, 90 KV) at the National Museum of Denmark, Copenhagen.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire X-ray images of sediment cores using a specified system

steps.description: The 40 cm-long images were merged to create one combined image of the cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Merge core image sections to create a single image per core

methods.description: High-resolution CT scanning of split core sections to obtain 3D imagery, followed by image processing (filtering, binning) and analysis (segmentation, counting) to quantify ice-rafted debris (IRD).

steps.description: a high-resolution CT scan of one-half of the ACDC2014-009 split core was undertaken using a ProCon Alpha Core CT scanner (Tube voltage 110 kV, current 800 mA, exposure time 250 ms) at EARTHLAB, University of Bergen. The scan was done in a continuous helix motion to produce 16-bit (65,536 grey values) 3D imagery with 53 µm voxel size (volume pixel).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire high-resolution CT scan of sediment core half
inputs: CT scanner (ProCon Alpha Core CT scanner)
scanning parameters (110 kV, 800 mA, 250 ms, helix motion)
desired output format (16-bit, 53 µm voxel size)

steps.description: We applied ring artefact and median filters
steps.searchable_summary: Apply ring artefact and median filters to CT scan data

steps.description: and reconstructed IRD with bins of 2 by 2 voxels to reduce the computational demand.
steps.searchable_summary: Reconstruct IRD from CT scan data using voxel binning

steps.description: To visualise the CT X-ray imagery, we used Avizo Fire software.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize processed CT scan data using Avizo Fire

steps.description: The watershed segmentation tool in Avizo Fire was used to isolate high-density particles with a diameter larger than 150 µm,
steps.searchable_summary: Perform watershed segmentation to isolate particles in CT data based on density and size

steps.description: and the number of these particles per 1 cm slice per depth in the core was used as a measure to quantify IRD.
steps.searchable_summary: Count segmented particles per depth slice

steps.description: and the number of these particles per 1 cm slice per depth in the core was used as a measure to quantify IRD.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify IRD based on particle counts per depth slice
outputs: IRD quantification data (e.g., IRD flux proxy)

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic susceptibility and acquisition of low-resolution X-ray images along the split core surface.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility (MS) at 1-mm intervals were measured on the split core surface with a Bartington MS2E point sensor
steps.searchable_summary: Measure magnetic susceptibility of sediment core at high resolution

steps.description: and low-resolution X-ray images taken using an Itrax Core Scanner at the Natural History Museum of Denmark.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire low-resolution X-ray images of sediment core using an Itrax Core Scanner

methods.description: Analysis of sediment samples to determine water content and grain size distribution.

steps.description: Samples for water content and grain-size analysis were subsampled every 1 cm continuously in both ACDC2014-009 and ACDC2014-010.
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample sediment cores at regular intervals for water content and grain size analysis

steps.description: Each aliquot was first weighed,
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh sediment samples (wet weight)

steps.description: then freezedried,
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze-dry sediment samples

steps.description: and then weighed again
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh sediment samples (dry weight)

steps.description: in order to calculate the percentage of water content.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate water content percentage from wet and dry weights

steps.description: The samples were rewetted in distilled water using a shaking table overnight
steps.searchable_summary: Rewet dried sediment samples for sieving

steps.description: before being manually wet-sieved through 63-and 150-µm meshes.
steps.searchable_summary: Wet-sieve sediment samples through specified mesh sizes

steps.description: All sample fractions were dried at 50°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry sieved sediment fractions

steps.description: Grains larger than 2 mm were manually separated from the >150-µm fraction to avoid a disproportional impact of larger grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Manually separate coarse grains (>2mm) from a sediment fraction

steps.description: The data are reported as weight percentages.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report grain size distribution as weight percentages

methods.description: Calculation of sediment mass accumulation rates (fluxes) using a formula incorporating sedimentation rate, water content, sample fraction percentage, and sediment density.

steps.description: Sediment fluxes (F, kg m −2 yr −1 ) were calculated using equation (1) (modified from Wangner et al., 2018), where S is the sedimentation rate (m yr −1 ), W is the water content in percent, P is the percent of the sample fraction and d is the density of quartz (2700 kg m −3 ): (1)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate sediment flux using a specified formula
inputs: percent of sample fraction (P, in percent)

methods.description: Processing of selected sediment samples by wet-sieving and preservation, followed by identification and counting of calcareous foraminiferal tests for paleoenvironmental analysis.

steps.description: Foraminiferal analysis was conducted on 12 samples from core ACDC2014-009.
steps.searchable_summary: Select samples for foraminiferal analysis from a sediment core

steps.description: One-cm slices were wet-sieved through a 63and 500-µm mesh
steps.searchable_summary: Wet-sieve sediment samples through specified mesh sizes for foraminifera

steps.description: and then stored in a solution of distilled water, ethanol and sodium bicarbonate to avoid carbonate dissolution (targeted pH ca 8).
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve sieved samples in a solution to prevent carbonate dissolution
inputs: preservation solution (distilled water, ethanol, sodium bicarbonate)

steps.description: Calcareous tests were identified and counted on a picking tray.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify calcareous foraminiferal tests

steps.description: Calcareous tests were identified and counted on a picking tray.
steps.searchable_summary: Count identified foraminiferal tests

steps.description: The results were plotted using the C2 software (Juggins, 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Plot foraminiferal analysis results using C2 software
